pagesAnalyzed.title: Linear – The system for product development

screenshot.note: Original screenshot exceeded provider image dimension limit; analysis used resized screenshot plus DOM extraction.

typography.sans: Inter Variable, SF Pro Display, -apple-system, system-ui, Segoe UI, Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, Open Sans, Helvetica Neue, sans-serif
typography.mono: Berkeley Mono, SF Mono, Menlo, monospace

spacing.majorSectionRhythm: one primary claim per viewport; long vertical gaps

patterns.name: Product surface is the hero asset
patterns.evidence: Hero pairs a short claim with a large Linear UI screenshot showing issue activity, labels, priorities, and agent chat.
patterns.dogHqTranslation: Use the actual Dog HQ dashboard as the landing-page proof: Today, meds, walk log, handoff note, and Dog Passport.
patterns.avoid: Mascot art, stock dog photography, or a lifestyle collage as the primary proof.

patterns.name: Monochrome frame, stateful color inside the system
patterns.evidence: Base #08090a and text #f7f8f8/#8a8f98 surround colored state chips inside product UI.
patterns.dogHqTranslation: Reserve warm and status colors for care states: overdue medication, completed walk, missed feeding, vet note, sitter handoff.
patterns.avoid: Using the Dog HQ accent on every card, heading, border, and icon.

patterns.name: Small controls signal operational seriousness
patterns.evidence: Nav links are 13px, controls are 32px high, product rows are around 28px, UI labels use 13px at weight 510.
patterns.dogHqTranslation: Make daily-care entries concise rows with timestamps, labels, and owner/sitter initials.
patterns.avoid: Oversized cards for every care event.

patterns.name: Whitespace separates ideas instead of dividers
patterns.evidence: The page is 10511px tall, with large isolated sections and few visible dividers.
patterns.dogHqTranslation: Give one section each to daily command center, health memory, sitter handoff, and Dog Passport.
patterns.avoid: Dense feature grids that turn the landing page into a settings screen.

patterns.name: Low-shadow depth
patterns.evidence: Shadow extraction is dominated by none; panels use 1px borders and surface steps.
patterns.dogHqTranslation: Style cards with dark fills, alpha borders, and small radii; keep shadows rare.
patterns.avoid: Large soft card shadows and glass highlights on routine panels.

principles.trigger: Care logs, medication, handoffs, vet records, and Dog Passport screens.
principles.decision: Lead with structured product UI: rows, timestamps, labels, status chips, initials, and concise notes.
principles.reason: Dog HQ must make owners trust that nothing gets forgotten; operational evidence is stronger than decoration.
principles.evidence: Linear hero UI shows issue activity, labels, priorities, cycle, project, and agent chat with 13px labels and 28px rows.
principles.tradeOff: Do not turn the landing page into a pet scrapbook; use dog identity and warmth as support.

principles.trigger: Color decisions across landing, dashboard, cards, and status chips.
principles.decision: Use near-black surfaces and neutral text; reserve Dog HQ warm color for active actions, overdue states, and one emotional callout.
principles.reason: Scarce color becomes meaningful and helps status information stand out.
principles.evidence: Linear uses #08090a background, #f7f8f8 primary text, #8a8f98 secondary text, #e5e5e6 CTA, and state colors mostly inside product UI.
principles.tradeOff: Do not use warm peach/pink as wallpaper across all surfaces.

principles.trigger: Marketing sections that need to explain MVP value quickly.
principles.decision: Pair a large short headline with a dense but controlled product crop underneath.
principles.reason: The headline carries the promise while the UI details prove maturity.
principles.evidence: Hero headline is 64px/64px with -1.408px letter spacing; supporting text is 15px/24px; UI below uses 13px rows and controls.
principles.tradeOff: Do not explain every feature in the hero.

principles.trigger: Cards, task rows, passport panels, and handoff notes.
principles.decision: Use 1px alpha borders, dark surface steps, and 6-12px radii before using drop shadows.
principles.reason: The interface reads as precise software and keeps dense information scannable.
principles.evidence: DOM extraction found shadows overwhelmingly set to none; borders include rgba(255,255,255,0.05) and #23252a; radii cluster at 6px, 8px, and 12px.
principles.tradeOff: Avoid large soft shadows, glass highlights, and raised panels unless a modal needs elevation.

antiPatterns: Do not clone Linear's brand mark, exact copy, or purple identity.
Do not make Dog HQ emotionally cold; keep warmth in copy, microcopy, one accent color, and useful status feedback.
Do not use dog photos as the primary interface texture.
Do not create a dense feature matrix on the landing page.
Do not use color as decoration; every colored chip should mean a care state.
Do not use heavy shadows for routine cards.
notes: This extraction translates structure and restraint; it should not copy Linear's brand identity.
Dog HQ should keep a warmer voice than Linear because the domain is care and trust, not enterprise velocity.
Recommended implementation target is DESIGN.md plus docs/taste/linear-for-dog-hq.md for detailed rationale.
